text_excerpt: --> About | Simon Lightwood MP top of page Home About Advice & Support Visit Parliament Gallery Transport Contact More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. About. About Simon Election Results Wakefield MPs Leeds MPs Constituency About Simon Simon Lightwood is the Labour and Co-operative Member of Parliament for Wakefield and Rothwell. ‚Äã He was first elected the MP for Wakefield in a by-election held on 23 June 2022 with a majority of 4,925 (17.9%) winning the seat back for the Labour Party, following the resignation of the disgraced Conservative MP, Imran Ahmad Khan. ‚Äã ‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã In Parliament he has campaigned on a wide range of issues, including bus services, NHS dentistry and antisocial behaviour in line with the priorities he set out in his election campaign. ‚Äã In October 2022 he was asked to join the Labour’s front bench as a Shadow Transport Minister, which included responsibility for buses, taxis and coaches. ‚Äã In September 2023 he was re-appointed as the Shadow Minister for Local Transport with an expanded portfolio to include buses, taxis, light rail, active travel, future of transport and Transport For London. Simon was re-elected for the new constituency of Wakefield and Rothwell on 4 July 2024 with a majority of 9,246 (22.9%). He was then invited by the new Prime Minister, Sir Keir Starmer MP, to serve as Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Minister for Local Transport) in the Department for Transport. ‚Äã The Prime Minister asked Simon to continue to serve as Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State ( Minister for Roads and Buses) in the September 2025 reshuffle. ‚Äã Prior to being elected, Simon was a proud NHS worker and was previously a Labour councillor and cabinet member for public health. ‚Äã He studied theatre acting at Bretton Hall College in Wakefield, after moving to the city in the late nineties. He bought his first home and met his husband whilst living in Wakefield. ‚Äã Simon lives in Wakefield with his husband and their two young children. He is a proud Wakefield Trinity fan. Downloadable portraits Election Results ‚Äã 2024 General Election: ‚Äã ‚Äã ‚Äã ‚Äã ‚Äã ‚Äã ‚Äã ‚Äã ‚Äã ‚Äã ‚Äã ‚Äã ‚Äã ‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äã 2022 Wakefield By-Election: Wakefield MPs ‚Äã ‚Äã ‚ÄãSimon is considered both a Wakefield and Leeds MP. ‚Äã Your MP is determined by where you live. The UK is divided into 650 constituencies, each represented by one MP. Your MP can only assist people who live within their own constituency, so it's important to make sure you're reaching out to the correct MP for your address. You can enter your postcode here to check. There are actually four MPs which cover the Wakefield. ‚Äã ‚Äã Simon Lightwood MP - Wakefield and Rothwell: Includes Rothwell, Stanley and Outwood East, Wakefield East, Wakefield North, Wakefield West, and Wrenthorpe and Outwood West. Yvette Cooper MP - Pontefract, Castleford and Knottingley: Covers Airedale and Ferry Fryston, Altofts and Whitwood, Castleford, Central and Glasshoughton, Knottingley, Pontefract North, and Pontefract South. Jade Botterill MP - Ossett and Denby Dale: Includes Denby Dale, Horbury and South Ossett, Kirkburton, Ossett, Wakefield Rural, and Wakefield South. Jon Trickett MP - Normanton and Hemsworth: Covers Ackworth, North Elmsall and Upton, Crofton, Ryhill and Walton, Featherstone, Hemsworth, Normanton, South Elmsall, and South Kirkby ‚Äã ‚Äã Leeds MPs ‚Äã ‚Äã ‚ÄãSimon is considered both a Leeds and a Wakefield MP. ‚Äã Your MP is determined by where you live. The UK is divided into 650 constituencies, each represented by one MP. Your MP can only assist people who live within their own constituency, so it's important to make sure you're reaching out to the correct MP for your address. You can enter your postcode here to check. There are actually ten MPs which cover the Leeds: ‚Äã Simon Lightwood MP - Wakefield and Rothwell Includes Rothwell, Stanley and Outwood East, Wakefield East, Wakefield North, Wakefield West, and Wrenthorpe and Outwood West. ‚Äã Hilary Benn MP - Leeds South Includes Beeston & Holbeck, Middleton Park
